page.title: Marbella Sunset Cruise with Romantic Dinner: A Perfect Evening

data.meta_description: Experience a marbella sunset cruise romantic dinner in Puerto Banús. Sail at 7 PM, dine under the stars. Book your evening charter from €749. Plan your perfect evening now.
data.summary: A marbella sunset cruise romantic dinner combines a private boat charter from Puerto Banús with a catered meal as the sun sets over the Costa del Sol. Choose from our fleet of 18 boats, with prices starting at €749 for a 2-hour evening charter.
key_facts: Departures from Puerto Banús at 7 PM for sunset
Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39)
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT
Romantic dinner catering available on request (ask via WhatsApp)
Fleet ranges from 5-pax speedboats to 12-pax luxury yachts
Sunset times vary seasonally; golden hour lasts ~45 minutes
Anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for a private dinner
Book via WhatsApp for custom menus and dietary needs

faq.q: What time does a sunset cruise typically depart?
faq.a: Most sunset cruises from Puerto Banús depart at 7 PM, allowing you to catch the golden hour as the sun sinks behind La Concha mountain. In summer, sunset is around 9:30 PM, so a 7 PM start gives you a relaxed sail before the main event. We adjust departure times seasonally; confirm when booking.

faq.q: Can we have a romantic dinner on board?
faq.a: Yes, we can arrange a romantic dinner on any charter. Simply let us know your preferences via WhatsApp—we’ll coordinate with local caterers for a menu that suits you. Options include seafood platters, paella, or a three-course meal. The dinner is served at anchor in a quiet cove like Cala del Faro.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Romantic dinner catering is an additional cost; contact us for a quote.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a romantic sunset cruise?
faq.a: For couples, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (both 12.5m, up to 11 pax) are ideal—they’re spacious yet intimate, with a flybridge for sunset views. For a more exclusive feel, the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) offers a sporty elegance. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: How long is a sunset cruise?
faq.a: We recommend a minimum of 2 hours for a sunset cruise, which gives you time to sail out, anchor for dinner, and return. Many guests book 3-4 hours to enjoy the full transition from dusk to night. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on Tier A boats.

faq.q: Can we swim during the sunset cruise?
faq.a: Absolutely. We anchor in sheltered coves like Cala Cortés or Cala del Faro, where the water is calm. Bring swimwear if you fancy a dip before dinner. The water temperature in summer averages 24°C, perfect for a sunset swim.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Your safety is paramount. If the forecast shows high winds or rough seas, we’ll reschedule your charter or offer a full refund. The skipper monitors conditions closely and will advise you 24 hours before departure. Most evenings on the Costa del Sol are calm.

faq.q: How do I book a marbella sunset cruise romantic dinner?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, boat type, and any dinner requests. We’ll confirm availability and send a payment link. For Tier A boats, the price is fixed; for others, we’ll provide a custom quote. Book at least 48 hours in advance.

data.body_html:
Picture this: the sun melts into the Mediterranean, painting the sky in shades of amber and rose, while you sip chilled cava on the deck of a private yacht. A marbella sunset cruise romantic dinner is more than an evening out—it's a memory crafted from golden light, calm seas, and exceptional food. Departing from the glamorous Puerto Banús, our fleet of 18 boats offers the perfect setting for an intimate night on the water.

Why Choose a Sunset Cruise in Marbella?

Marbella's coastline, backed by the Sierra Blanca and La Concha mountain, creates a natural amphitheatre for sunset. The light reflects off the sea, and the evening breeze cools the air. A private charter lets you escape the crowds of the Golden Mile and enjoy the view from the water. With a licensed skipper at the helm, you can relax, dine, and watch the stars emerge.

Our Fleet for Romantic Evenings

We have boats to suit every couple's style. For an intimate two-hour escape, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal—both depart from Puerto Banús and cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, including skipper, drinks, snacks, and VAT. For a longer evening, the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) offers sporty elegance, while the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) provides a spacious flybridge for panoramic sunset views. All charters include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Romantic dinner catering can be arranged via WhatsApp.

What to Expect on Your Evening Charter

Your skipper will greet you at Puerto Banús at your chosen time (typically 7 PM). After a safety briefing, you'll cruise westward along the coast, passing the Marbella Marina and the exclusive villas of the Golden Mile. The boat will anchor in a quiet cove—Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés—where you can swim, dine, or simply watch the sunset. The golden hour lasts about 45 minutes, but the afterglow continues for another hour. You'll return to port under a canopy of stars.

Romantic Dinner Options

We partner with local caterers to offer a range of dinner menus. Choose from a seafood platter with grilled prawns and lobster, a traditional paella Valenciana, or a three-course meal with starter, main, and dessert. Dietary requirements (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free) are accommodated. Simply message us on WhatsApp with your preferences, and we'll arrange everything. Dinner is served at anchor, with the boat gently rocking as you eat.

Prices and Booking

Prices for a sunset cruise start at €749 for 2 hours on Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39). For larger yachts like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), the minimum charter is 4 hours at €4,719, which includes a free jet ski. All other boats are priced on request—contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote. Dinner catering is additional; we'll provide a detailed cost based on your menu. Book at least 48 hours in advance to secure your preferred boat and time.

What to Bring

Pack light: swimwear, a towel, sunglasses, and a light jacket or wrap for the cooler evening breeze. Sunscreen is essential even for sunset—the sun's rays are still strong. If you're celebrating a special occasion (anniversary, proposal, birthday), let us know and we can arrange flowers, decorations, or a cake.

Nearby Destinations to Explore

While sunset cruises typically stay close to Marbella, you can extend your charter to visit Estepona (8 NM west) or Sotogrande (15 NM west) for a longer evening. The skipper can tailor the route to your preferences. For a daytime prelude to your sunset dinner, consider a half-day charter to Cabopino or Marbella Marina.

Frequently asked questions

What time does a sunset cruise typically depart?

Most sunset cruises from Puerto Banús depart at 7 PM, allowing you to catch the golden hour as the sun sinks behind La Concha mountain. In summer, sunset is around 9:30 PM, so a 7 PM start gives you a relaxed sail before the main event. We adjust departure times seasonally; confirm when booking.

Can we have a romantic dinner on board?

Yes, we can arrange a romantic dinner on any charter. Simply let us know your preferences via WhatsApp—we’ll coordinate with local caterers for a menu that suits you. Options include seafood platters, paella, or a three-course meal. The dinner is served at anchor in a quiet cove like Cala del Faro.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Romantic dinner catering is an additional cost; contact us for a quote.

Which boats are best for a romantic sunset cruise?

For couples, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (both 12.5m, up to 11 pax) are ideal—they’re spacious yet intimate, with a flybridge for sunset views. For a more exclusive feel, the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) offers a sporty elegance. All depart from Puerto Banús.

How long is a sunset cruise?

We recommend a minimum of 2 hours for a sunset cruise, which gives you time to sail out, anchor for dinner, and return. Many guests book 3-4 hours to enjoy the full transition from dusk to night. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on Tier A boats.

Can we swim during the sunset cruise?

Absolutely. We anchor in sheltered coves like Cala Cortés or Cala del Faro, where the water is calm. Bring swimwear if you fancy a dip before dinner. The water temperature in summer averages 24°C, perfect for a sunset swim.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Your safety is paramount. If the forecast shows high winds or rough seas, we’ll reschedule your charter or offer a full refund. The skipper monitors conditions closely and will advise you 24 hours before departure. Most evenings on the Costa del Sol are calm.

How do I book a marbella sunset cruise romantic dinner?

Contact us via WhatsApp with your preferred date, boat type, and any dinner requests. We’ll confirm availability and send a payment link. For Tier A boats, the price is fixed; for others, we’ll provide a custom quote. Book at least 48 hours in advance.
